title: Audit the current self-improvement loop with adversarial learning cases
summary: Added a reproducible red-team harness and report showing that the current learning scorer is vulnerable to keyword stuffing and can incorrectly push Charlie into exploratory mode after bad answers.
changes: Added deterministic self-improvement red-team audit script.
Documented the current learning pipeline end-to-end.
Recorded measured false positives for keyword stuffing and contradictory reasoning.
Confirmed that bad answers can still yield exploratory delegation mode if the scorer is fooled.
risks: Learning-driven delegation remains vulnerable until scorer hardening is implemented.
Current learning scores should not be treated as a high-trust proxy for operator understanding.
rollback: Remove the audit harness/report if analysis artifacts need to be separated from production docs; no runtime behavior depends on them.
